cells: Spell goes off, but caster's swell of pride is punished by the loss of twice the normal PP.
Caster's faith could be stronger. Spell goes off, but RRs have a +20 bonus.
Perseverance is rewarded. Caster's spell is delayed until next round.
Caster completes spell, but effort is exhausting. Caster suffers a fatigue penalty.
Music of the Spheres distracts the caster. Spell must be cast again from the beginning (but no PP are lost).
Caster falters, losing the spell and half the normal PP (round up).
Caster's faith wavers for just a moment. The spell is lost, but not the PP.
Patience, my child. This spell will be cast next round.
Caster conceives a complex theological argument against casting this spell. Spell and half PP (round down) are lost. Caster is stunned [-50] one round.
Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round down). Frogs rain down in a 10'R around the caster, leaving him stunned [-25] for two rounds.
Caster learns something, but it has nothing to do with what he was trying to learn.
The next detail of the spell slips the caster's mind and the caster loses the whole thing (but keeps the PP).
Caster loses spell and the normal number of PP; be thankful it was no worse.
Caster's invocations are not answered. Nothing happens, except that the PP for the spell are gone.
Instead of the desired information, the caster gets a brief lesson in theology. +10 to next religion-related Lore/ Science maneuver, but spell fails.
The power of the caster's god is near, though the caster made a minor error. Caster may spend twice the normal PP and save the spell, or lose the spell and spend no PP.
This reminds me of the parable of the caster who lost his spell and the PP to cast it. Because that is what happens.
Fate smiles on the target. The spell has no effects, and the caster loses twice the normal number of PP.
The information comes, but in obscure, symbolic, metaphoric terms.
The wise are humble. This spell-casting has no effect other than the caster's loss of one PP.
Spiritual power runs a little wild. Caster takes 1d10 hits and three rounds of stun [-50].
Caster is enthusiastic, but careless. Spell fails and takes three times the normal number of PP with it.
The caster learns only of a good place to seek the knowledge through mundane means.
Caster suffers a moment of existential crisis. Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round down). Caster may not cast or prepare any spells for 3 rounds.
Flowers bloom at the caster's feet, but uncontrolled power also stuns [-50] the caster for three rounds, does 2d10 hits, and turns any non-magical item in the caster's hands to salt.
Target must have a guardian spirit. Caster is stunned [-50] for seven rounds.
Caster has a glimpse of coming doom! Caster is stunned [-50] for one round.
Botched spell creates a sense of nausea. Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round up) and suffers a -5 fatigue penalty.
Caster manages to smite himself, taking a B Holy crit and gets stunned [-50] two rounds by embarrassment.
Caster accidentally invokes the wrong name. Penance is 4d10 hits and 24 hours of unconsciousness.
Caster gains the desired information, but has alerted one enemy to his exact location.
Caster mishandles the spell energies, doing himself 1 hit of damage per PP invested in the spell. Spell and PP are lost. Caster is stunned [-25] for 2-20 rounds.
Caster accidentally heals himself of 1d10 hits, which stuns [-50] him for three rounds.
Caster takes 3d10 hits from misdirected energies as spell targets alternative target(s) closest to intended target.
Nothing can be seen but the caster's ultimate insignificance. Caster is stunned [-50] for 3 rounds.
Caster loses spell and PP. Caster has no idea why, leaving him stunned [-50] for 3 rounds.
Caster accidentally does 1d10 hits damage to himself, stunning him [-50] for two rounds.
Caster accidentally targets (an) alternative target(s) closest to the caster. Caster will be at -5 to all SCRs until he spends a day in religious observations.
Caster gains information relevant to his power source's interests, but not to his own inquiry. Caster spends two rounds stunned [-25].
The spell does nothing. Surprise leaves caster stunned [-50] for 2 rounds, then stunned [-25] for 2 more.
Somehow caster manages to tap into the wrong power source. It burns! Caster takes a C critical and 5d10 hits.
Spell targets randomly (a) victim(s) within 500' (ignore normal range). Caster will be at -20 to all SCRs until spending an entire day in religious observations.
Caster sneezes loudly. Spell unravels, taking with it twice the usual PP.
Caster tries to fix mistakes, making them worse. Caster takes 3d10 hits and is stunned [-75] for 5 rounds.
Caster's wild treatment of the elements damages his connection to his power source. He will be at -50 and unable to regain PP until he spends a week in religious observances while on sacred ground.

That was almost blasphemous. Caster takes 1 hit per level of spell, collapses into unconsciousness for 1d10 hours and loses all spell-casting ability until spending a full week in religious observations.
Caster hears the whispered lies of the spiritual opposition and takes 5d10 minutes taking no action but cleansing his mind of these voices.
Caster forgets key element of spell and it shatters, leaving the caster at -30 to all actions for 1d10 hours.
Caster sees too deep into the fathomless cosmos and falls into a two week coma. However, for the week after that he gains a +20 bonus to all Lore & Science skills.
Caster commits a major faux pas and targets himself. RR applies, but so does -50 “willing target” penalty (and range is touch).
Caster gains one piece of true, but misleading, information. Any money he is carrying will have disappeared.
Caster disrupts his own aura. All spells on caster are dispelled, even curses. Caster's RRs are at -50 until he spends a week in religious observances on sacred ground.
Spell actually goes off as planned, but caster takes an A critical. Saved from far worse by divine grace, the caster is placed under a Quest spell to repay the favor.

Caster summons more power than he can handle. He bursts into flames, doing an E Heat crit to himself and a C Heat crit to anyone in a 10'R. Caster keeps taking C Heat crits each round until fire is extinguished.
Caster beholds the glories of his deity. Caster is overwhelmed and falls into a 1d10 day coma.
Caster is bathed in unmanageable power. Caster takes a B critical and loses all spell-casting ability for 1d10 weeks.
Caster takes a D crit as he loses control, then takes another 5d10 hits from internalized energies.
Caster is struck blind, which also causes him five rounds of stun [-50].
Caster gains a random vision before mishandled energies rebound on him for 6d10 hits, 6 rounds of stun [-50], and 6 days at -40.
Caster is overcome by sloth. Caster will do nothing but act in self-defense, talk, or move at a walking pace for 1d10 days. Caster has only half normal PP for 1d10 weeks.
Caster takes an E critical and loses his spell-casting abilities until he spends a month in religious observances on sacred ground.
Spell exposes stain on the caster's soul. Caster loses all spell-casting ability until he fulfills a quest from his deity or religious superior.
Caster has a terrible, haunting vision. Caster will be at -30 until he spends a week in religious observances on sacred ground. Also, all his PP are gone.
Caster is struck dumb for 1d10 weeks as spell energies swirl around his head. He also takes 5d10 hits and 1d10 hours of enchanted sleep.

Caster burns with too much divine power, taking two E Holy crits. Everyone not of his faith within 50' of the caster takes a C Holy crit. Caster turns to ash. Anyone not of his faith and within 20' of the caster takes an E Holy crit.

Caster experiences severe strain: -50 Fatigue penalty, loss of all PP, aged one year, and at a -30 penalty that recovers at a rate of -1 per day spent in religious observations. Caster is a mortal servant of the divine. Caster proves it by suffering a heart attack and dropping dead. 41

Caster's errors lead to damaging the tie to his deity. Caster is at -10 and cannot cast spells until spending two weeks in religious observances. Caster has visions of Armageddon. This results in brain shut-down. Caster is in a coma for 3 months.

Caster loses connection to his power source. Shock delivers 10d10 hits and 12 rounds stunned [-75]. Spell-casting is impossible until caster spends 1 month in religious observances on sacred ground. Caster offends the higher powers and is struck by a +100 Lightning Bolt.
Caster gets the spell off, but excess power internalizes, doing 3d10 hits.
Essence swirls aimlessly. Spell may be cast next round.
Minor ripples in the Essence require more care to achieve success. Spell may be cast next round.
Spell functions normally, but excess Essence energy does 1d10 hits to the caster.
Caster's fingertips spark, surprising him. Spell and one PP are lost. Caster is at -50 next round.
When you push the Essence, sometimes it pushes back. Spell goes off, but caster is stunned [-50] for five rounds.
The Essence bends to those who remember all the words to a spell. Caster loses the spell (but not the PP).
Caster's technique is inefficient, but if he devotes another PP to this spell, he can still get it off.
Minor power backlash. Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round down) and is stunned [-50] one round.
Essence feedback blurs caster's vision, causing him to lose the spell and half the normal PP (round down). Caster is stunned [-50] for one round.
Confusing, conflicting data leaves caster stunned [-25] next round and still as ignorant as when he started.
That was not how the caster was taught to execute this spell. Caster loses the spell, but keeps the PP.
Caster catches a mistake before it becomes catastrophic and aborts the spell, losing the normal number of PP.
Caster loses his place in the spell and it all unravels taking the PP with it into oblivion.
Caster picks up the wrong thread of the Essence and gets the right information about a similar, but different, target.
Caster suddenly realizes he is casting the wrong spell. The spell is lost, but not the PP.
Caster has a choice to make: finish the spell next round and take 1d10 hits per level of the spell or simply abort the spell and waste the PP.
Spell has no effect. The caster loses twice the normal PP. The Essence is fickle.
Caster gains partial information (if possible), but the process is tiring, resulting in a -30 fatigue penalty.
The Essence twitches slightly. Caster loses the spell and 1 PP.
Elemental energies ripple across caster's torso, doing 5 hits and three rounds of stun [-50].
Power backlash! The spell fails completely. Caster is stunned [-50] for 8 rounds.
Caster obtains information that is mostly incorrect.
A muscle cramp in the caster's jaw causes the spell to fail. Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round down). Caster is stunned [-50] for 2 rounds.
The elements refuse the caster's call, knocking him back 3 feet, doing 2d10 hits and six rounds of stun [-50].
Caster is overwhelmed by painful power internalization, collapsing to the ground, taking 1d10 hits, and suffering nine rounds of stun [-50].
For a moment, caster glimpses the full complexity of the local Essence. Caster is stunned [-50] for two rounds, but gains a +10 to his next SCR made within the next 5 minutes.
Caster's fingers go all wiggly. Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round down). Caster drops anything in his hands.
Elemental power is summoned, but not properly directed. Caster takes 20 hits and a B critical.
Essence flows through the caster, carrying away all of his PP.
Information gained is, so far as possible, exactly the opposite of the truth.
Caster internalizes the spell. Caster takes 2d10 hits and is stunned for 2d10 rounds. Spell and PP are lost.
The Essence seems especially stubborn today. Caster is stunned [-50] for two rounds.
Spell targets the nearest alternative target(s) to the intended target(s). Caster is stunned [-50] one round.
Information gained is garbled beyond sense. Caster's frustration gives -20 penalties to SCRs for the next hour.
The spell strays off target and vanishes, leaving the caster stunned [-50] for 2 rounds.
Caster gets confused by chaotic behavior of the elements and is stunned [-50] for three rounds.
Spell targets the alternative target(s) nearest to the caster. Caster takes 1d10 hits and 1 round of stun [-50] from mental strain.
Caster gains useless information about an unknown target. In addition, there is so much information, caster is stunned [-50] for 3 rounds.
Caster watches spell do nothing and spends three rounds stunned [-50] pondering why.
Power? Yes. Control? No. Caster takes a B crit this round and an A crit next round.
Spell target(s) chosen randomly from those within 500' (ignore normal range). Spell backlash causes caster 25 hits and leaves him stunned [-25] for 1d10 hours.
Caster strains too hard to get the information. Spell fails utterly; caster takes 5 hits and is stunned [-50] for 3 rounds.
The spell misfires badly. Caster takes 1d10 hits and is stunned [-50] for 1d10 rounds.
Essence surges and burns the fringes of the caster's aura. Caster cannot regain PP for a month.
Complete confusion leads caster to cast a random spell at a random target. Shock puts caster into a coma for 1d10 days.
Caster obtains false, but plausible, information.
Caster's aura spasms as Essence is mishandled. Caster loses any attunements to magic items, takes 4d10 hits, and is stunned [-50] for 8 rounds.
Caster manages to cast the spell (centered) on himself. Caster takes normal damage, as does anyone else affected. Caster also falls into a 1 week coma.
Spell misfires and internalizes inside caster's head. Caster takes 35 hits and loses spell-casting ability for 1d10 weeks. There is a 40% chance of a 1d10 day coma.
Essence is misdirected by the caster, who takes 3d10 hits and an A critical.
Severe power feedback knocks caster down, doing 1d10 hits and an A Impact critical. Caster is unable to function for 1d10 hours.
There seems to be some confusion about which element the caster hoped to command, and he takes 3 different C criticals.
Caster suffers a massive stroke! This causes 10d10 hits and a month-long coma. At the end of the coma, caster seems fine, but will die 1d10 hours later unless heart has been mended.
Essence is reshaped around the caster, disrupting his aura and putting his RRs at a -50 penalty for 1d10 hours.
Caster is drained of all PP, takes 10d10 hits, and is stunned [-75] for 12 rounds, after which exhaustion delivers a -50 fatigue penalty.
Caster takes 1d10 rounds of stun [-50] and a C crit from wild elemental power, then loses his remaining PP, taking a hit of damage for each one.
Caster internalizes spell energies; resulting nerve damage causes permanent reduction of PP by 50% (unless entire nervous system is healed).
Caster peers into the deep mysteries of the Essence. This awesome vision puts caster into a 9 week coma, but he wakes automatically attuned to any magic items on his person.
Caster sends Essence through his legs, leaving him unable to use them for two weeks. He also takes 30 hits and 3 rounds prone & stunned [-50].
Caster takes 3d10 hits and an E critical; caster blinded for 2d10 rounds.
Spell completely internalizes. Caster loses all spell-casting ability for 1d10 weeks, will be in a coma for 1d10 weeks, and takes 8d10 hits.
The spell collapses, releasing uncontrolled energies. Caster takes 20 hits, is knocked out for 9 hours, and loses spell-casting ability for 4 weeks.
Caster internalizes the spell, taking 4d10 hits and spending 2d10 hours knocked out. Caster loses all spell-casting ability for 1d10 weeks.
Elemental power surges through caster, doing 1-5 E crits and putting caster in a coma for 1d10 weeks. Elemental energies consume the caster, leaving a charred skeleton behind.
Severe nervous disorder causes caster to lose all spell-casting ability for 1d10 months. Massive internalization of spell causes heart and brain to simultaneously shut down. 42

Caster gains the desired information. With luck, this will still matter after his month-long coma. Caster appears to have confused Informational magic with Elemental magic and takes 6d10 hits and an E critical.

Caster is stunned [-50] for 1 round per level of the spell, takes 6d10 hits, falls into a coma for 1d10 days, and has a -50 penalty to all SCRs for 2d10 weeks. Caster's sensitivity to the Essence is damaged, causing a -100 penalty to all Power Manipulation, Delving, and Spellcasting skills (and -50 to SCRs) for one year.
Caster's focus wavers, but he can complete the spell next round.
Target stirs a troubling thought in the caster's mind. Caster loses the spell (and one PP).
Caster gains the desired information… but so does everyone else in a 20' radius.
Caster successfully casts the spell, but a low-intensity headache puts caster at -10 for all actions for the next hour.
Caster loses the spell due to lack of focus. Caster can only take defensive actions for the next two rounds.
Caster remembers a childhood incident that was traumatizing. Caster loses the spell (but not the PP). Caster operates at -10 for 5 rounds.
A moment of distraction causes the caster to lose the spell (but not the PP).
Caster's focus strays. Spell is delayed for one round, but it is cast normally next round.
Caster loses spell, half the PP (round down), and 1d10 hits to a brief, but intense headache.
Caster's negative thoughts prevail. Spell is lost along with half the PP (round down). Caster takes 3d10 hits.
Caster gains partial information (if possible) and cannot attempt to study this subject again until tomorrow.
A twinge of headache causes caster to lose the spell (but not the PP) and 1 hit.
Caster realizes he was thinking of a cake recipe, not a spell. Spell is lost, along with the PP.
Caster seeks the mental state to complete the spell, but cannot find it. Spell and all PP are lost.
The information gained is erroneous… but so absurd that spell failure is sure to be suspected.
Caster suddenly remembers some mundane detail he had forgotten. Unfortunately, he loses his spell (but not the PP).
Spell was visualized perfectly, but caster forgot to conceptualize a target. The PP vanish without producing an effect.
Caster strains to save the spell, but it fails. All PP are lost and caster suffers a -20 fatigue penalty.
The information gained is mostly correct, but some inaccuracy is included.
The power of the caster's mind recalls a great joke. Caster laughs loudly and the spell is lost (but not the PP).
Error literally takes caster's breath away, doing 10 hits, one round stunned [-75], followed by one round stunned [-25].
Spell has no effect other than triggering an overwhelming headache, leaving the caster at three rounds of stun [-75].
Caster learns something completely different than what was sought, leaving him stunned [-50] for 1 round.
Caster relaxes his concentration too soon. The spell and the PP are both lost.
Caster's mind wanders into obsessive contemplation of elemental physics, resulting in four rounds of stun [-75].
Caster finds struggle to impose his will exhausting. Caster suffers 2d10 hits, a -40 fatigue penalty, and 4 rounds stunned [-25].
Caster learns something about himself, leaving him stunned [-25] for two rounds.
Backlash stuns the caster for 2 rounds. Caster loses the spell and half the normal PP (round up).
Caster just cannot bend matter to his will. Trying too hard makes him faint for 1d10 minutes of unconsciousness.
The spell internalizes. Caster takes 4d10 hits and is knocked unconscious for 12 hours.
Caster learns one piece of very specific and incorrect information. Power feedback does 1d10 hits as well.
A major headache leaves caster stunned [-50] for 10 rounds. Spell and PP are lost.
Caster gets a stress headache, leaving him at -20 for 1d10 minutes.
Spell targets an alternative target at random from among those not allied to the caster. Confused, the caster spends three rounds stunned [-25].
Caster's subconscious must want this information kept hidden. Befuddling images stun [-50] caster for two rounds.
Spell fades away, taking the invested PP with it. This is depressing enough to leave the caster at a -10 penalty for the next five minutes.
Caster accidentally directs the spell at the alternative target nearest to himself. This stuns [-50] the caster for 1 round.
Target is selected at random from those friendly to the caster. Embarrassed, the caster also takes two rounds of stun [-50].
Caster learns what some random target thinks is the truth about what he wanted to know. Caster is stunned [-25] for 3 rounds.
This spell simply fails, taking the PP to nowhere. Self-doubt gives a -10 to caster's SCRs for the rest of the day.
Caster pulls elemental energies towards himself too strongly, takes a C critical, and expends twice the normal PP for this spell.
Mental strain blanks entire spell list from caster's mind. He will recover memory at 1 rank per day. Bewildered, caster is at -50 for 1d10 hours.
Information comes in garbled and stuns [-50] caster for 5 rounds. However, caster will get the information properly in 1d10 days in a dream.
Caster manages to stun [-50] himself for 5 rounds and glows for 1d10 minutes.
The elements cannot be controlled! Caster's brain is fried (loses all spell-casting ability for 1 week). Caster operates at -50 for 3 months (or until brain is repaired).
Target is selected at random within 500' range (ignore normal range). Caster's brain is scrambled, leaving him at -75 for 1d10 months (or until brain is repaired).
Caster obtains false, but plausible, information.
Spell misfires in caster's head. Caster is knocked down, taking 2d10 hits, and is unable to function for one hour.
Caster mishandles energies badly and takes 9 rounds of stun [-50]. Caster cannot attempt this spell again until gaining another rank in the relevant spell list.
Brain strain causes complete PP drain. Caster is unable to cast spells for 2d10 weeks and is stunned [-50] for six rounds.
Caster stumbles onto a revelation of his own character flaws. Caster is at -100 for the next hour.
As the spell spirals out of control, the spell-casting part of the caster's brain shuts down for self-preservation. Caster is unable to cast spells for 1d10 days.
Caster takes a D crit, is unable to cast spells for a week, and develops a phobia of whatever he was targeting.

The spirit is willing, but the body proves weak. Caster's skin splits, causing him to bleed 10 hits/rnd. Meanwhile, caster is stunned [-75] 10 rounds, during which he cannot cast spell subconsciously.
Caster has a vision of his own death! Caster collapses and is unconscious for 1d10 hours, after which he wakes suffering from paranoia.

Caster is flung against the nearest solid object for 5d10 hits, is knocked out for 12 hours, and loses spell-casting ability for 1d10 weeks. Caster develops compulsion to never take any penalty to an SCR for this spell.
Caster overloads his nervous system, taking 10d10 hits and losing the ability to cast Elemental spells for 1 year (or until nerves are repaired). Then the elemental power hits for a D crit.
The spell completely short circuits caster's nervous system, leaving him paralyzed from the waist down. Caster also goes bald.
The victim of a mild stroke, the caster takes 4d10 hits and suffers a -50 to any Endurance rolls made for the next week plus 1d10 days.
Caster suffers a mild stroke, taking 20 hits and a -50 penalty lasting 1d10 days. He also cannot cast spells for 2 weeks.
Caster gains an incomprehensible glimpse into the elemental planes and retreats into a 1d10 month coma.
Pain floods through the caster, doing 10d10 hits and 10 rounds of stun [-50]. Caster will be at -25 due to headaches for 1d10 weeks.
Caster mixes up his own mind and is unable to cast spells, or use Lore/ Science skills other than his native tongue, until he spends a week in undisturbed meditation.
Caster damages his psyche, giving a -100 penalty to SCRs. Penalty decreases by -1 per day or -2 per day spent in peaceful meditation.
Caster takes an E crit and 8d10 hits, spends 1d10 minutes stunned [-50], and loses all spell-casting ability for 3 months. Caster demonstrates that spontaneous decapitation is not just a myth.
Massive feedback strain puts caster into a coma for 1d10 months. Caster's skull explodes, causing instant death. 43

Caster now has a nervous tic. Caster is stunned 1d10 rounds. Casting from this spell list will have a -20 SCR penalty (until caster is psychologically healed). Caster reveals his mind to everyone within 100'; they can read his thoughts for 1d10 rounds, after which he loses spell-casting ability for 6 months.

Caster is stunned [-50] for 2d10 rounds and must relearn this spell in order to cast it again. Caster also develops a mild mental illness (GM's option). Caster suffers a severe stroke and drops into a 1d10 month coma.
